channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Korea's impeached president defies warrant after hourslong standoff
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators left the official residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after a near-six-hour standoff on Friday during which he defied their attempt to detain him.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 dead and 19 injured in Southern California plane crash
item.description: FULLERTON, Calif. (AP) — Two people died and 19 were injured Thursday when a small plane crashed through the roof of a sprawling furniture manufacturing building in Southern California where at least 200 people were working, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to adjust your budget if you're unemployed
item.description: More than a quarter (26%) of households live "paycheck to paycheck," spending over 95% of their income on necessities.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Carter's quest for Mideast peace didn't end with Camp David
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — As president, Jimmy Carter brokered the watershed peace agreement that removed Israel’s most powerful enemy from the battlefield. But he incurred the Israeli government's wrath decades later when he said its military rule over the Palestinians amounted to apartheid.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Korean investigators attempt to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators arrived at the presidential residence with a warrant to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived imposition of martial law, but faced resistance from presidential security staff as hundreds of Yoon’s supporters gathered outsid
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: An aspiring nurse, a football star, a single mother and a father of 2 killed in New Orleans attack
item.description: An 18-year-old woman dreaming of becoming a nurse, a single mother, a father of two and a former Princeton football star were killed when the driver of a white pickup truck sped down Bourbon Street, packed with holiday revelers.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC protesters follow New Orleans attack by calling for 'intifada revolution' hours after rampage
item.description: Terror suspect Shamsud-Din Jabbar flew an ISIS flag as he plowed through New Year's Eve revelers in New Orleans. Hours later, New York demonstrators called for an "intifada revolution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI found 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home during search in December, prosecutors say
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Federal agents found one of the largest stockpiles of homemade explosives they have ever seized when they arrested a Virginia man on a firearms charge last month, according to a court filing by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US unemployment claims fall to 211,000, the lowest level since March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The number of Americans applying for unemployment checks dropped last week to the lowest level since March, suggesting that suggest most U.S. workers continue to enjoy unusually high job security.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Soldier in Las Vegas Cybertruck explosion had gunshot wound to head: sheriff
item.description: The man who drove a Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside of a Las Vegas, Nevada hotel has been identified as Matthew Livelsberger, an Army soldier from Colorado, a law enforcement source told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, Fox 5

item.title: Trump blames ‘open borders’ for New Orleans, Las Vegas attacks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump blamed the situation at the U.S.-southern border for the recent attacks in New Orleans and Las Vegas, railing against President Joe Biden for his immigration policies as both cases are being investigated.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI says it thwarted apparent plot against AIPAC
item.description: The FBI thwarted an apparent plot to harm employees at a Florida office of the American Israel Public Affairs Committee.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: 10 people shot outside New York City nightclub on New Year's Day: NYPD
item.description: At least 10 people were shot outside a Queens nightclub on New Year's Day, according to the New York City Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KRON4

item.title: A drop in groundwater could mean big trouble for a tiny snail as Nevada lithium project presses on
item.description: RENO, Nev. (AP) — Opponents of the nation's largest lithium mine under construction want U.S. officials to investigate whether the project in Nevada already has caused a drop in groundwater levels that could lead to extinction of a tiny snail being considered for endangered species protection.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT SONNER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Feds probe possible accomplices in NOLA terror attack as thousands prep for Sugar Bowl and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYPD says 'not a terrorist attack' after 10 shot outside NYC event space on New Year's Day
item.description: At least 10 people were injured by gunfire late Wednesday at the Amazura nightclub in Jamaica, Queens, New York City police confirmed to Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI says driver in New Orleans rampage acted alone and was '100%' inspired by Islamic State group
item.description: The FBI now says that the pickup truck driver responsible for a deadly rampage in New Orleans acted alone.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Jim Mustian, Kevin Mcgill And Jack Brook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An aspiring nurse, football star, single mother and father of 2 killed in New Orleans attack
item.description: An 18-year-old girl dreaming of becoming a nurse, a single mother, a father of two and a former Princeton football star suffered fatal injuries when the driver of a white pickup truck sped down Bourbon Street, packed with holiday revelers early Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Broccoli sold at Walmart stores in California, 19 other states could cause sickness: FDA
item.description: A fresh vegetables and salad company has issued a precautionary advisory for some washed and ready-to-eat broccoli florets distributed at Walmart stores in 20 states, including Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy the Baptist: Carter redefined ‘evangelical,’ from campaigns to race and women’s rights
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — Before reaching the 1978 peace deal between Egypt’s Anwar Sadat and Israel’s Menachem Begin, Jimmy Carter managed months of intense preparation, high-stakes negotiations at Camp David and a field trip to the Gettysburg battlefield to demonstrate the consequences of war.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Broccoli sold at Walmart stores in 20 states could cause sickness: FDA
item.description: A fresh vegetables and salad company has issued a precautionary advisory for some washed and ready-to-eat broccoli florets distributed at Walmart stores in 20 states, including Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KRON4

item.title: Here are 5 hot-button laws taking effect in 2025
item.description: States across the country are bracing for new laws set to take effect as early as Wednesday, including some that have stirred controversy.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why you should probably throw away your Christmas tree this week
item.description: With the holiday season now behind us, it might be time to consider losing your Christmas trees, too.
item.dc:creator: Tylor Sorensen, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman burned to death in NYC subway identified by police
item.description: The woman was set on fire and killed while likely asleep on a stationary train at the Stillwell Avenue subway station on Dec. 22, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, Fox 5

item.title: US Treasury says Chinese hackers remotely accessed workstations, documents in 'major' cyber incident
item.description: The department said it was working with the FBI and the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency and others to investigate the impact of the hack, and that the hack had been attributed to Chinese state-sponsored culprits.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AP WAS THERE: Carter announces Middle East peace agreement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — ___ EDITORS: As part of its coverage of the death of former President Jimmy Carter, The Associated Press is looking back at major events in his life. Carter's success at Camp David negotiating a Mideast Peace agreement between Egypt and Israel was one of his crowning achievements.
item.dc:creator: BARRY SCHWEID, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter sought to expand democracy worldwide long after he left the White House
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — Amid everything else on his desk — the Iran hostage crisis, domestic economic turmoil, the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan and a grueling 1980 reelection fight — President Jimmy Carter elevated the independence of a country in southern Africa as a top agenda item.
item.dc:creator: FARAI MUTSAKA and CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court upholds verdict finding Trump liable for sexually assaulting E. Jean Carroll
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Monday upheld a jury’s verdict finding President-elect Donald Trump liable for sexually assaulting advice columnist E. Jean Carroll and ordering him to pay $5 million.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Move over, Gen Alpha: 2025 marks the start of the Gen Beta era
item.description: If you just found out about Generation Alpha, brace yourself for a new crop of kids set to debut on Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Sakshi Venkatraman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Census Bureau releases 'population clock' projection for New Year's Day
item.description: Based on current estimates, there will also be one birth every 9 seconds and one death every 9.4 seconds in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, ABC 4

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US this New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year. The sun expelled two bursts of plasma that are hurtling toward Earth and are expected to arrive early this week, according to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gal Gadot says she had 'massive blood clot' in her brain during pregnancy
item.description: "Wonder Woman" actor Gal Gadot revealed Sunday that she underwent emergency surgery earlier this year after a "massive blood clot" was found in her brain while she was eight months pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5
